description: Trigger discrimination for the Phase 7.5 agentic cluster: containment (ASI08 cascade + ASI10 rogue/drift) vs cost guardrails, incident execution, goal hijack, telemetry implementation, and the generated-code sandbox.

cases.prompt: Design the blast-radius isolation, drift detection, and kill switches for our fleet of autonomous agents so one bad agent can't take the rest down.
cases.reason: Fault propagation, drift detection, inventory, and authority-severing kill switches are the merged ASI08+ASI10 containment review; cost caps are composed from the guardrail designer and incident execution belongs to the runbook.

cases.prompt: Add token caps, per-tenant AI budgets, and burn-rate alerts before our LLM bill gets away from us.
cases.reason: Consumption/spend guardrails (LLM10) are the cost designer's; containment composes those budgets into cascade control but owns fault and drift, not the caps themselves.

cases.prompt: One of our agents is actively spamming customers right now — walk us through severity, roles, containment, and comms.
cases.reason: Running a live incident (severity ladder, roles, containment execution, comms) is the incident runbook, which invokes the kill-switch design this skill produced; the review does not run incidents.

cases.prompt: Attackers are redirecting what our agent works on via content it ingests — keep its objective from being altered.
cases.reason: Adversary-directed objective alteration is ASI01; containment's ASI10 half covers deviation without active attacker control, plus the detection/kill machinery either case needs.

cases.prompt: Wire up the dashboards and alert rules for our agents' error rates and latency in our monitoring stack.
cases.reason: Hands-on implementation of alerts/dashboards is the observability operator (manual-only); containment defines WHAT baselines and drift signals must exist and hands the wiring over.

cases.prompt: Review the sandbox around the Python our agent writes and executes so generated code can't escape or reach secrets.
cases.reason: The execution sandbox for agent-generated code is the LLM05/ASI05 output-handling surface; containment bounds failure spread BETWEEN agents, not the code-exec sink inside one.
